article_title: The first experience with fully endoscopic posterior cervical foraminotomy and discectomy for radiculopathy performed in Viet Duc University Hospital
label: Figure 1
caption: The position of a patient during surgery.

article_title: The first experience with fully endoscopic posterior cervical foraminotomy and discectomy for radiculopathy performed in Viet Duc University Hospital
label: Figure 2
caption: Under fluoroscopic control, the guide wires are inserted through the posterior cervical musculature with the tip directed to the operative disc space.

article_title: The first experience with fully endoscopic posterior cervical foraminotomy and discectomy for radiculopathy performed in Viet Duc University Hospital
label: Figure 3
caption: Working channel Insertion. On a string conductor, tubular reamers of increasing diameter were introduced, on which a working cannula with a beveled cut with an external diameter of 7.5 mm was installed.

article_title: The first experience with fully endoscopic posterior cervical foraminotomy and discectomy for radiculopathy performed in Viet Duc University Hospital
label: Figure 4
caption: Bone Resection. Laminotomy and facetectomy are performed with 3.0 mm diamond boron, from “V-point” to the periphery.

article_title: The first experience with fully endoscopic posterior cervical foraminotomy and discectomy for radiculopathy performed in Viet Duc University Hospital
label: Figure 5
caption: Preparation and removal of the herniated material.

article_title: The first experience with fully endoscopic posterior cervical foraminotomy and discectomy for radiculopathy performed in Viet Duc University Hospital
label: Figure 6
caption: Surgical diagrams for Endoscopic Posterior Cervical Foraminotomy and Discectomy techniques: A and B localize of working cannula. C The facet joint is identified by removing the overlying soft tissue: (1) Facet joint (2) yellow ligament. D Final operative view: (1) nerve root (2) disc herniation (3) Facet joint (4) yellow ligament.

label: Figure7
caption: Neck pain intensity on visual analogue scale (VAS- Neck) before and after surgery. Minimal clinically important differences (MCID) = 28 mm.

article_title: The first experience with fully endoscopic posterior cervical foraminotomy and discectomy for radiculopathy performed in Viet Duc University Hospital
label: Figure 8
caption: Arm pain intensity on visual analogue scale (VAS- Arm) before and after surgery. Minimal clinically important differences (MCID) = 26 mm.

label: Figure 9
